title: Four parts, directions superimposed progressively — organic

rule.instruction: A square is divided horizontally and vertically into four equal parts, each with lines in four directions superimposed progressively.

restrictions: Faithful structure: the square is divided into four equal quadrants; the four kinds of lines are superimposed PROGRESSIVELY across the parts — one part has 1 direction, the next 2, the next 3, the last all 4 (a density progression, light->dark). BUT executed entirely through the governing organic taste (R8): every part is HAND-DRAWN (not-straight, jitter ~3-7, the living-line sweet spot), FINE/DENSE spacing, and ASYMMETRIC (each quadrant wobbles on its own seed; the progression is arranged irregularly, not a tidy ramp). No clean/crisp/symmetric renderings (explicitly disliked). The 16 span the organic range: gentle->wild hand, finer/denser, and varied progression arrangements + base direction orders.
